name: Universo
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a display font family, inspired by retro-futurist poster design and old scientific publications, with strong influence from Aldo Novarese's mid-century work. The stencil variant and headline-focused intent confirm its display classification.
summary: A retro-futurist display family in six styles, designed for a Chilean social-commentary fanzine and shaped by Aldo Novarese's mid-century Italian type.
description: Designed by Mauro Andrés under the creative direction of Victoria Rivas, Universo draws on retro-futurist poster aesthetics and the kind of scientific publications that Aldo Novarese worked alongside in the 1950s. The family runs across five upright weights from Thin to Black, with a Stencil variant rounding out the six styles. Conceived for headlines, concert posters, magazine covers and branding, it includes a wide set of OpenType features including stylistic alternates, ligatures and oldstyle figures.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Universo as a display family created for "Caos Sagrado," a fanzine built around exposing social problems. The name references the phrase "we have to talk about the horrible things of Universe." Designed between 2018 and 2020, it was first shown publicly at Impresionante, an independent printing expo in Chile, before being completed in six styles under Peggo Fonts co-production.

name: PGF-Elyss
classification_notes: A multi-subfamily display project drawing on calligraphic, Lombardic and Nouveau traditions; the Roman, Script, Catchwords and Ornaments variants all serve decorative, heading-level use rather than extended text setting.
summary: An Art Nouveau-inflected display family spanning a Roman, Lombardic, Script, Catchwords and Ornaments set, inspired by Jean Larcher's calligraphic work.
description: Developed over eighteen months, the family grew from a single Roman into five distinct subfamilies united by an organic, Art Nouveau sensibility. The Roman runs in four weights from Light to Bold, the Script includes a Small variant, and both the Catchwords and Ornaments sets follow the same four-weight structure. Positioned at labels, headers and cover design, it covers over 200 Latin-based languages and includes an extensive OpenType feature set with swash, small capitals and stylistic alternates.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF-Elyss as a multi-style project that began as a simple font idea and grew over a year and a half into a full family. Inspired by Jean Larcher's calligraphic work and shaped by an Art Nouveau organic aesthetic, it brings together a Roman, Lombardic, Elegant Script, Catchwords and Ornaments set, making it suited to label, header and cover design with support for over 200 Latin-based languages.

name: PGF-Orqquidea
classification_notes: The Manuscrita subfamily is rooted in handmade calligraphy by Marcela Aguilera, making it a calligraphic script; the broader family also includes a geometric sans and decorative/ornament variants, but the defining voice of the family is its handwritten script character.
summary: A calligraphic family built from handmade lettering by Chilean calligrapher Marcela Aguilera, extended into a sans, decorative and ornament subfamilies by Pedro González.
description: Orqquidea spans 12 variants across four distinct subfamilies: a five-weight geometric sans, a five-weight calligraphic Manuscrita ranging from Hair to Contrast, and two decorative additions in Garden and Deco. The Manuscrita subfamily draws directly from Aguilera's physical calligraphy work, while González's type design extends that into a full system suited to packaging, labels, branding and editorial layouts. OpenType features include standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates, oldstyle figures and fractions.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF Orqquidea Manuscrita as based on handmade calligraphy by Chilean designer, calligrapher and teacher Marcela Aguilera (Lanascribe), complemented by illustration and type design work from Pedro González. It forms part of the broader Orqquidea family, which includes a Sans, a Script, ornament and decoration sets, and typographic resources designed for labels, packaging, editorial work, retail graphics and branding.

name: LeBrush
classification_notes: Described as a contemporary Roman typeface based on real brush lettering with proportions drawn from Capitalis Monumentalis, placing it firmly in the display category with a brush-drawn serif character.
summary: A brush-lettered display roman in ten weights, drawing its proportions from the Capitalis Monumentalis of the Trajan Column for use in titling, posters and book covers.
description: Built from real brush lettering rather than constructed forms, LeBrush interprets classical Roman monumental proportions through an expressive, hand-drawn lens. Ten weights run from Thin to ExtraDark, with the Classic weight positioned specifically for movie titling, magazine covers and poster work. An extensive OpenType feature set includes standard, discretionary and historical ligatures, swash alternates, stylistic sets and ornamental extras, making it usable in logotypes and labels where detail matters.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe LeBrush as a contemporary Roman typeface grounded in real brush lettering, inspired by the classical proportions of the Capitalis Monumentalis found on the Trajan Column and Greek architectural structures. The Classic weight was developed specifically for movie titling graphics, book and magazine covers, and posters, while advanced OpenType options and ornamental sets extend its use into logotypes and labels.

name: PGF-Americas
classification_notes: Inspired by Rudolf Koch's Carved Letter design artwork, PGF-Americas is a decorative display family intended for headlines, posters, labels and cover design rather than extended text use.
summary: A decorative display family by Pedro González, drawing from Rudolf Koch's Carved Letter tradition across ten weights plus inline, initials and ornamental variants.
description: Developed between 2015 and 2021, the family spans ten upright weights from Thin to ExtraDark alongside two Inline cuts, a dedicated Initials set, an Ornaments variant and a weather-themed Dingbats set. The proportions and rhythm remain consistent across all 15 variants, making it practical to mix decorative and functional cuts within a single composition. It targets label design, book and film cover work, posters and educational materials, with an extensive OpenType feature set including swash, discretionary ligatures, multiple stylistic sets and a full range of figure styles.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF-Americas as a readable, playful and versatile family inspired by Rudolf Koch's Carved Letter artwork, offering a weight range from Thin to ExtraDark plus two Inline weights, an Initials set, ornaments and weather-themed dingbats. They position it as an expressive tool for labels, book and film covers, headlines, posters and friendly educational products, supported by a generous OpenType feature set.

name: PGF-Business
classification_notes: Explicitly modelled on Spencerian penmanship, historically known as 'Business Script', with confident signature-style strokes. Categorised as script due to its calligraphic, cursive nature.
summary: A Spencerian-inspired script family organised into five stylistic variants, a comprehensive full file, and supplementary ornament and dingbat sets.
description: Drawn from the tradition of Spencerian penmanship and confident signature lettering, PGF-Business is split into five individual style files for ease of use, with a more feature-dense 'Full' variant intended for users comfortable with advanced OpenType editing. The family ships with an extensive set of stylistic sets, ligatures, swashes and figure styles, alongside separate Ornaments and Dingbats fonts for typographic decoration.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF-Business as inspired by map scripts, Spencerian 'Business Script' and confident manual signature strokes. The family is organised into five individual styles for simpler use, plus a comprehensive 'Full' file offering far more options for those with advanced editing skills, accompanied by Dingbats and Ornaments sets for versatile typographic compositions.

name: Sharka
classification_notes: A heavy, sharp, condensed display family explicitly designed for high-impact headlines and extreme visual contexts; the aggressive angular forms and compressed widths place it firmly in the display category with no text-setting intent.
summary: A heavy condensed display family in 21 variants, built around sharp angular forms intended for aggressive headlines and high-impact advertising.
description: Sharka spans seven width variants from Extra Condensed to an extra-poster cut, each available in upright, italic and alternate versions for a total of 21 fonts. The italic cuts are designed to convey speed and mechanical energy, while the alternate set offers stylistic variation for the same weight and width pairings. Aimed squarely at magazine covers, advertising alerts and action-oriented editorial work where size and impact take precedence over legibility at small scales.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Sharka as a heavy sharp condensed system of seven display widths plus italics and alternative versions for each, inspired by the dangerous personality and aggressive reputation of the great white shark. Intended to convey high impact, high risk and powerful mechanical energy, they recommend it for big headlines, magazine covers, advertisements and public visual calls, noting it can also suit web and digital media when applied with typographic care.

name: PGF-Strange
designer: Pedro González (graphic interpretation & production), Bruno Jara (concept)
classification_notes: A chromatic/multilayer Roman display face with sharp edges and compact proportions, designed for stacking decorative layers to achieve volumetric and retro effects; firmly in the display category.
summary: A multilayer chromatic display face in eight decorative layers, drawing on 1970s–80s lettering traditions with sharp edges and compact proportions.
description: Built from eight stackable variants — Outline, Marquee, Star, Inline, Base, Vol-Shade, Volume and Shadow — the family is designed to be combined for dimensional, retro-volumetric effects in branding, packaging and headline work. Sharp edges and compact proportions give it a period flavour without tipping into pastiche. OpenType support includes stylistic alternates, old-style figures, ligatures and case-sensitive forms.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF-Strange as a multilayer Roman font with eight levels, inspired by 1970s–80s aesthetics, with sharp edges and compact proportions aimed at a contemporary retro volumetric style. They position it for branding, packaging, logotypes, headlines, signage and 3D lettering, with a full suite of OpenType features including stylistic alternates, ligatures, old-style numbers, fractions and localised forms.

name: Rationell
classification_notes: Rationell draws on 1950s Swiss rationalism with underlying structure referencing 19th-century Didone modernism, placing it firmly in the neo-grotesque tradition with humanist Latin inflections.
summary: A 24-style corporate sans drawing on mid-century Swiss rationalism with a Didone structural underpinning, covering 222 Latin-based languages across 12 weights and matching italics.
description: Spanning Hairline to ExtraBlack with matching italics, the family is sized for both continuous text and monumental display applications such as billboards and building-scale printed mesh. Forty OpenType features include contextual alternates for 'Il' disambiguation, automatic Roman numerals, old-style and tabular figures, and localised forms across more than 2,200 glyphs. The design targets corporate publishing, wayfinding and complex information systems where consistency across weight extremes matters.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Rationell as a functional multipurpose corporate typeface rooted in classic 1950s Swiss rationalism and subtly structured on a 19th-century Didonesque modernist skeleton, interpreted through Latin idiosyncrasy. The family's average weights serve text sizes in print and digital media, while the extreme weights address headlines, posters and building-scale applications. With 40 OpenType features and support for 222 Latin-based languages across more than 2,200 glyphs, they position it as a voice for corporate publishing, branding, wayfinding and complex technical information systems.

name: Milica
classification_notes: Milica is squarely a display typeface designed with straight verticals, slanted horizontals and no curves, drawing from action movie and urban military aesthetics — placing it firmly in the display category with a militaristic, angular subcategory.
summary: An angular display typeface with straight verticals and slanted horizontals, built for poster, film and urban graphic contexts.
description: Designed with hard, sharp strokes and no curves, Milica draws from action movie and military visual culture to produce a distinctly blunt typographic voice. The family covers five weights from ExtraLight to ExtraBlack, each with a matching italic, and ships with OpenType features including ligatures, oldstyle figures and stylistic alternates. Suited to large-scale print, video game titles, animation and hip-hop-adjacent graphic work.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Milica as a display font inspired by action movies and urban military culture, constructed with straight verticals, slanted horizontals and no curves. It comes in five weights with matching italics and is recommended for posters, film, video games, TV, animation, magazines, POP culture, hip-hop topics and large-format print.

name: Primaria
classification_notes: Primaria is classified as display due to its playful, teaching-inspired character; it draws directly from primary school handwriting instruction, offered in both cursive and print styles.
summary: A primary-school handwriting revival offered in both cursive and print styles across three weights, with OpenType alternates to improve letter connections.
description: Drawn from the basic cursive and print letterforms taught in early education, Primaria comes in six variants split across two subfamilies: a Cursive Pack and a Print Pack, each in Fina, Regular and Gruesa. OpenType features include standard and discretionary ligatures, three stylistic sets and ordinals to smooth awkward letter pairs. Best suited to children's products, educational materials, food and drink packaging and soft goods branding.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Primaria as a display font inspired by the handwriting styles taught at primary school, available in cursive and print forms across three weights each. The design accounts for typographic and stylistic needs, with OpenType initial lowercase alternates to improve linking between letter pairs. They recommend it for playful and friendly design, teaching and learning projects, children's toys, food and drink, and soft goods.

name: PGF-Dinos
classification_notes: A low-contrast rounded display face inspired by Block Sign Paint lettering, with a distinctly playful and decorative character that places it firmly in the display category rather than any functional text category.
summary: A rounded, low-contrast display face drawn from Block Sign Paint lettering traditions, with inverted asymmetric proportions that give each line of text a lively, top-heavy rhythm.
description: Originally named Globa, PGF-Dinos was designed by Pedro Gonzalez and released between 2014 and 2015. Its defining characteristic is an asymmetric letter structure where the upper half of each character appears larger than the lower, with distinctive bending finials visible on letters such as C, G and S. The family spans ten weights from Hairline to ExtraBlack, each with a matching italic, and is positioned toward sport products, food packaging, video games and entertainment media.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF-Dinos as a low-contrast rounded display typeface with a ludic look, inspired by the Block Sign Paint lettering style. Its inverted asymmetric proportions make the upper half of each letter appear larger than the lower, creating an attractive effect along the reading line. Traditionally associated with sign painting, label and branding design, it is primarily recommended for sport products, food, video games and entertainment media. Distribution and reviews are handled by LatinoType.

name: PGF Posters
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a display font for poster design with high-density spacing and thin counter forms; its primary function is titling and typographic backgrounds rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight display face built for poster work, using tightly spaced letterforms with thin counters to fill space and interact with imagery.
description: Designed specifically for poster and typographic background use, it relies on a high glyph density to pack into layouts and sit alongside photographs or graphic elements. Despite the narrow counter forms, the foundry claims usable legibility for most titling contexts. Two stylistic sets and a slashed zero are the only OpenType features on offer, keeping the toolset modest.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe PGF Posters as a display font built for poster design, with a high-use density spacing that fills space and creates eye-catching typographic backgrounds capable of interacting with images and other visual communication elements for a modern look. They note that despite thin counter forms it maintains sufficient legibility for titling use.

name: Fonton
classification_notes: Fonton is classified as a display typeface owing to its large, volumetric letterforms inspired by barrel shapes, with soft curves designed explicitly for poster and large-format use rather than continuous text.
summary: A barrel-inspired display typeface from Peggo Fonts, built for poster and large-format work with soft, volumetric curves and OpenType decorative features.
description: Drawn with reference to the rounded form of large barrel shapes, Fonton uses soft curves across each stroke to create a contemporary display face suited to posters, magazine covers and POP graphics. The family ships in three variants, Extralight, Poster and Small, and includes discretionary ligatures, alternates and stylistic figures enabled via OTF features. Its urban, hip-hop aesthetic makes it a functional choice for large-scale print and graphic culture work.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Fonton as a contemporary modern display font inspired by the shape of large ton barrels, intended for poster work, covers, headings, letterheads and big-size prints. The family covers two weight sizes plus an Extralight variant, and includes decorative ligatures, alternates and figures all sharing the same graphic style, powered by OTF technology.

name: Röijer
classification_notes: Röijer is a display typeface drawing on Lombardic and Art Nouveau influences, with dual capital models and volumetric variants that place it firmly in the decorative display category rather than text use.
summary: A decorative display typeface with Lombardic and Art Nouveau influences, offering dual capital models and a volumetric variant for branding and luxury applications.
description: Designed by Pedro González under the creative direction of Marcela Aguilera, Röijer provides two sets of capitals per letter: a formal Roman style in the lowercase box and a more glamorous alternative in the uppercase box. The family ships in four variants including a volumetric cut alongside ornament and dingbat sets, each containing 541 glyphs with discretionary ligatures, oldstyle figures and full fraction support. Positioned for branding, label design and headline work in fashion and luxury contexts.
foundry_blurb: Peggo Fonts describe Röijer as born from a high-care branding exercise, developed with designers Marcela Aguilera and Pedro González, treating each letterform as a typographic jewel that balances classical and contemporary ideas with Lombardic and Art Nouveau references. The typeface offers two capital models per letter and two main variants of 541 glyphs each, accompanied by ornament and decorative object sets, suited to branding, titling, label design and luxury work.
